_metadata.description: Validates agent-tasks correctness, repair causality, and verification sharpness using synthetic repos modeled after flask and httpx benchmark data.

webapp.description: Flask-like web app with MDS + PFS + DIA patterns

mutant_duplicate: Exact duplicate: _make_timedelta in app.py and base_app.py
pattern_fragmentation: error_handling: 4 variants in handlers/ (try/except, custom exc, result-dict, assert)

repairs.repair_description: Fixed README: removed phantom dir refs (webapp/, handlers/, models/)
Standardized all handlers: identical except Exception + raise pattern

failure_cases.failure_analysis: Renaming a function does not resolve MDS. Drift uses body hashes, not names. Identical body still triggers detection.

failure_cases.repair_description: Removed original phantom refs but added config/, migrations/, static/ (phantom)
failure_cases.failure_analysis: Removing original phantom dirs but adding new phantom refs (config/, migrations/, static/) still triggers DIA. Drift checks all directory references, not just previously flagged ones.

datalib.description: httpx-like data lib with MDS + EDS + SMS patterns

explainability_deficit: Unexplained complexity: transform_records (CC>=12, 6 params, no docstring)
system_misalignment: Novel deps: native_optimizer.py uses numpy/pandas/pyarrow in data lib

repairs.repair_description: Extracted flush() into BaseDecoder, eliminated duplication

repairs.side_effect_note: Targeted finding resolved, but 1 finding(s) of type 'explainability_deficit' remain (new or pre-existing non-targeted findings).

failure_cases.repair_description: Added trivial docstring (complexity unchanged — EDS persists)
failure_cases.failure_analysis: Adding a one-line docstring does not resolve EDS when the function retains high cyclomatic complexity (CC>=12) and 6 untyped parameters. Drift detects structural explainability deficit, not just missing docstrings.

datalib_sms.description: datalib with date-split history for SMS detection

system_misalignment: Novel deps: native_optimizer.py uses numpy/pandas/pyarrow in data lib

repairs.repair_description: Replaced numpy/pandas/pyarrow with pure-Python — aligns with module conventions

failure_cases.repair_description: Added justification comments (novel deps unchanged — SMS persists)
failure_cases.failure_analysis: Renaming the file does not remove novel dependencies. Drift tracks novel imports, not file names.

apiserver.description: API server with layering violation (DB imports API)

architecture_violation: Upward layer import: db/user_repo.py imports from api/serializers.py (layer 2 -> layer 0)

repairs.repair_description: Removed upward import from DB layer — returns raw data

failure_cases.failure_analysis: Aliasing an import does not change the dependency direction. Drift detects the layer violation via resolved import paths, not symbol names.

churnapp.description: App with high-churn config_loader for TVS detection

temporal_volatility: High churn: config_loader.py modified 8+ times in 30 days while other files have 1-2 commits

repairs.repair_description: Deleted config_loader.py, split into config.py + config_schema + config_env

failure_cases.repair_description: Updated docstring only — file remains monolithic high-churn target
failure_cases.failure_analysis: Updating the docstring does not reduce churn. The file remains the single high-churn target. Drift tracks commit frequency, not content quality.

mutant_duplicate: Exact duplicate: _format_size in uploads.py and display.py
doc_impl_drift: Phantom dirs: middleware/, plugins/, docs/ referenced in README

repairs.repair_description: Fixed README: removed phantom middleware/ and docs/ refs

failure_cases.repair_description: Swapped phantom refs (deploy/, scripts/, monitoring/, infra/) — DIA still triggers

mutant_duplicate: Exact duplicate: _parse_header in binary.py and streaming.py
explainability_deficit: Unexplained complexity: format_report (CC>=10, 6 params, no docstring)

repairs.repair_description: Split format_report into _transform + _build_row helpers with docs

failure_cases.repair_description: Added trivial docstring (complexity unchanged — EDS persists)

proven: Deterministic repair-task generation from analysis findings
Controlled verification: correct repairs reduce drift scores
Rejection sharpness: incorrect repairs are not falsely accepted
Task schema completeness and priority ordering
Reproducibility: identical input produces identical output
Signal coverage: MDS, EDS, DIA, PFS, AVS, TVS, SMS verified
Real-world repair verified on httpx (MDS, DIA)
not_yet_proven: Real coding agents executing tasks autonomously in production repos
Multi-step repair orchestration across dependent findings
Comparative advantage over unguided agent repair (control group)
known_limitations: TVS side-effects: repair commits create temporal_volatility findings as a side effect. The TVS cascade test shows these stabilize and do not diverge, but sequential agent repairs must account for this.
Sample sizes: n=12 correct, n=10 incorrect. FAR/FRR are directionally valid but not yet statistically robust (need n>=10 per class).
EDS repair reduces score but may leave a residual finding (e.g. high parameter count). targeted_finding_resolved tracks this precisely.

summary.conclusion: Translation + Verification benchmark: agent-tasks produce valid, correctly prioritized repair tasks across 7 signal types. Correct repairs measurably reduce drift scores. Incorrect repairs are rejected. Deterministic across repeated runs. Signal coverage: 7/7. TVS side-effects tracked and stable.

tvs_cascade_test.conclusion: TVS findings appear as side effects of repair commits but do not diverge. Sequential repairs do not create an unbounded TVS cascade.

repairs.description: DIA: README missing tests/ directory → added reference
